title: Chain-of-thought prompting: why it works so well
post:
Chain-of-thought prompting sounds like buzzword. Here's why it actually works:

When AI models 'think step by step,' they break complex problems into intermediate steps. This prevents them from jumping to conclusions and allows errors to be caught earlier.

Research shows this simple technique dramatically improves performance on reasoning tasks. Not because the model becomes smarter—but because it externalizes its reasoning process.

You can see where it went wrong. You can verify the logic. You can course-correct mid-problem.

For complex tasks, always ask for step-by-step reasoning. For simple tasks? Save the overhead—direct answers work fine.

The key is matching the prompting style to the complexity of the task.

What prompting techniques have worked best for you?
